pubmed_links: [Shen et al. (2010) Mutations in PNKP cause microcephaly, seizures and defects in DNA repair](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20118936/); [Aceytuno et al. (2012) Impact of PNKP mutations on enzyme activity and DNA strand break repair in microcephaly/seizures/developmental delay](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22508754/); [Thuresson et al. (2024) Novel PNKP mutations with reduced DNA single-strand break repair and severe microcephaly, seizures, and developmental delay](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37916443/)
summary: PNKP encodes a DNA repair enzyme (polynucleotide kinase-phosphatase) whose loss-of-function mutations cause microcephaly with early-onset seizures and developmental delay (MCSZ), a severe neurodevelopmental syndrome, as well as ataxia-oculomotor apraxia type 4 and Charcot–Marie–Tooth disease type 2B2. Impaired DNA strand break repair in neurons underlies the progressive neurological deficits, with intellectual disability and refractory epilepsy as core features. (well-established)
